title: valerianella woodsiana var. patellaria
intro: Valerianella woodsiana var. patellaria is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Valerianella genus, commonly referred to as the 'campion' or 'corn salad' plant. It is an annual or biennial herb native to Europe, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory ailments, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Herbal preparations of Valerianella woodsiana var. patellaria include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties.
